feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: 18 Funny Women Who Made May Worth Every Aggravating Moment
entry.summary: "Can my boyfriend come?' Can my flesh-eating virus come?"

entry.title: Dispute grows over home monitoring of hantavirus passengers
entry.summary: White House officials have been pushing for aggressive monitoring at home for those who return from the quarantine facility, but has retreated from initial proposals. Negotiations are ongoing with state officials.

entry.title: Fox Chapel woman’s battle with melanoma included immunotherapy
entry.summary: Christy Redican grew up in Connecticut, spending summers sailing and playing tennis. “I was a sun worshipper,” she said. “I did all the things you’re not supposed to do. I have fair skin, blonde hair and light eyes. I burned a lot.&rdq…

entry.title: WHO Chief Visits Bunia—Epicenter Of Africa’s Ebola Outbreak (Live Updates)
entry.summary: There have been more than 220 suspected deaths since the start of the Ebola outbreak.

entry.title: More than half of adults with ADHD in clinical settings have a co-occurring personality disorder
entry.summary: A new analysis suggests that 57 percent of adults seeking help for ADHD also meet the criteria for a personality disorder. Researchers found that conditions like avoidant and borderline personality are surprisingly common in this population.

entry.title: Cats could help scientists better understand human cancer, study says
entry.summary: A study of nearly 500 domestic cats uncovered similarities between cancer gene mutations in felines and humans.

entry.title: Here's What It Means If You Get Winded From Walking Up The Stairs
entry.summary: Most people have been out of breath after climbing the stairs — but is that normal?

entry.title: Inside Europe's largest Copper Age tomb, children's bones expose an ancient health crisis hidden for 5,000 years
entry.summary: Nearly 5,000 years ago, respiratory infections, possibly including tuberculosis, were ravaging the children buried at Camino del Molino (CMOL), Spain. The massive circular burial cave carved into rock is Europe's largest Copper Age mass burial, containing ove…

entry.title: A Texas woman thought her new diet was causing digestive issues. Doctors found cancer: "I couldn't believe it"
entry.summary: Candace Tucker thought her symptoms were benign. A colonoscopy led to an alarming diagnosis.

entry.title: Drinking These Beloved Beverages May Be Linked With A Lower Dementia Risk, Study Finds
entry.summary: Researchers evaluated participants for over 40 years and found interesting takeaways related to their dietary intake and cognitive health.

entry.title: 64 People Reveal Their Fascinating "Medical Mysteries" They Didn't Know Were "Uncommon" At All
entry.summary: "Apparently, some people have an 'internal monologue'?"

entry.title: Vibrio vulnificus has returned to Florida beaches
entry.summary: Florida has logged more Vibrio cases early in 2026 as the high-risk summer season approaches.

entry.title: Eating ice cream too fast? Brain freeze says a surprising amount about your health
entry.summary: If you’re having ice cream headaches often, it might be worth seeing if it runs in your family and taking a second look at your non-brain-freeze headaches too.

entry.title: Millions of breast cancer patients could safely avoid chemotherapy, study suggests
entry.summary: A new DNA test could help screen whether patients require the treatment or not, according to a new international trial.

entry.title: Mpox cases in Boston on the rise despite declining nationally
entry.summary: Between August 1, 2025, and March 31, 2026, 77 mpox cases were reported in Massachusetts, according to officials.

entry.title: Showing my hernia makes me less self-conscious
entry.summary: Amy Bamford has taken to social media to talk about her "massive" incisional hernia.

entry.title: Google planning to release millions of mosquitoes into California to help stop diseases
entry.summary: California could soon become a testing ground for one of Google’s most ambitious public health projects yet. The tech giant is seeking federal approval to release up to 32 million specially t…

entry.title: Former CDC director believes US will likely see Ebola cases
entry.summary: A new outbreak of Ebola, caused by the Bundibugyo virus, has spread undetected for weeks across Congo and into Uganda.

entry.title: Experts Say This Nighttime Habit Is Linked with Poor Liver Health
entry.summary: Experts share the top nighttime habit that may be harming liver health, along with other tips for protecting your liver.

entry.title: VDH reports measles case at St. Mary’s Hospital
entry.summary: The Virginia Department of Health is reporting a new measles exposure in Richmond.

entry.title: How learning to read alters the brain’s approach to spoken language
entry.summary: Learning to read does more than help you understand written text. A new brain imaging study reveals that formal reading education physically changes the neurological pathways humans use to process and decode spoken language.

entry.title: Two North Texas counties monitoring travelers for Ebola symptoms
entry.summary: Tarrant and Denton counties are checking in with travelers for three weeks after they return from Ebola‑affected countries.

entry.title: It’s Alive? Surprising Discovery Changes What We Know About Fog
entry.summary: Scientists discovered that fog droplets can host living bacteria that grow and help remove harmful pollutants from the atmosphere, revealing fog as a surprisingly active microbial environment.

entry.title: US adult cigarette smoking rate hits another all-time low
entry.summary: The cigarette smoking rate among U.S. adults dropped to another all-time low last year

entry.title: I Survived Breast Cancer. I Wasn't Prepared for What Came Next.
entry.summary: I just hit my 5-year breast cancer remission anniversary. These are the 4 hard things no one told me about being a survivor.

entry.title: Map reveals where norovirus is hitting hardest in US
entry.summary: A new national wastewater map is offering a window into the spread of the highly contagious norovirus across the United States.

entry.title: Tracking the 2026 Ebola outbreak in maps and figures: Locations, severity, how contagious it is and more
entry.summary: Learn more about the deadly 2026 Ebola outbreak, including how many cases there are so far, which countries are most affected, how this outbreak compares with others in severity and more.

entry.title: Rabid bat found in Snohomish County
entry.summary: In Snohomish County, six bats have tested positive over the last decade, but this week's bat is the second to test positive for rabies in Washington this year.

entry.title: There’s a Deadly ‘Rotating’ Soup of Forbidden Toxins at Lake Erie
entry.summary: Starting in early spring, Lake Erie's algae situation gets so toxic that officials make sure to monitor it—but it seems far from enough.

entry.title: A ‘Black Hole’ in Your Consciousness Makes You See a Unique Version of Reality, Neuroscientists Say
entry.summary: Turns out we’re not all watching the same version of the world unfold, just continuously updating our own personal “simulation.”

entry.title: A Biological Signature of Consciousness Found
entry.summary: Researchers discover a 20–45 Hz thalamic rhythm that serves as a biological signature of active states of consciousness.

entry.title: Stop Protecting Your Children—It’s Backfiring
entry.summary: Childhood is supposed to involve contact with the world itself. Modern parenting has forgotten that, writes Charlotte Grinberg.

entry.title: Low Folate and B12 Proven to Drive Chronic Fatigue
entry.summary: A new study links elevated homocysteine from B12 and folate deficits to physical fatigue in men and low motivation in women.

entry.title: 4 Things a Cardiologist Does Before Bed for Better Sleep
entry.summary: A cardiologist shares four nighttime habits that help improve sleep quality and duration.

entry.title: The High-Protein Mediterranean Diet Casserole I Can’t Stop Making
entry.summary: Creamy Chicken Florentine Casserole is packed with protein, whole grains and Mediterranean-inspired ingredients—making it a comforting, nourishing dinner you’ll want on repeat.

entry.title: Drug that grew extra teeth in mice enters first human trial
entry.summary: Researchers at Kyoto University Hospital have begun the first human trial of TRG-035, a drug meant to grow new teeth in people who are missing them. Humans keep a set…

entry.title: 3 receiving preventive treatment after exposure to rabid bat in Snohomish County home
entry.summary: Three Snohomish County residents are receiving preventive treatment for rabies after being exposed to a bat that tested positive for the virus, county health of

entry.title: 911 dispatchers rally around 4-year-old Rigby boy after brain tumor diagnosis
entry.summary: RIGBY — A group of 911 dispatchers is supporting a young boy from Rigby and his family after he was recently diagnosed with cancer. His name is Stetson Poole, and his mother, MeKenna Poole, is a Jefferson County 911 dispatcher. She has been working as a dispa…

entry.title: New Wearable Ultrasound Device Could 'Transform' Pregnancy Care
entry.summary: It's hard to imagine pregnancy care without the ultrasound.

entry.title: Kohl's employee with measles may have infected others at East Lampeter Township store [Update]
entry.summary: A Kohl’s department store employee infected with measles may have exposed others to the disease while at the 2350 Lincoln Highway East store in East Lampeter Township May 21, 23,

entry.title: Two Distinct Autism Subtypes Identified Via Brain Connectivity
entry.summary: A new study uses fMRI and mouse models to identify synaptic hypoconnectivity and immune hyperconnectivity autism subtypes.

entry.title: Think You’re Immune to Late-Day Caffeine? Your Sleep May Disagree
entry.summary: Recent research finds that caffeine can reduce the time we spend in deep sleep, even if it goes unnoticed.

entry.title: How To Get Rid of Fatty Liver Disease and Lose Weight Faster After 50
entry.summary: If you feel like the number on the scale keeps creeping up and your energy is dragging, your liver may be waving a white flag. Nearly a quarter of Americans...

entry.title: Class action lawsuit filed against Broome County and Sheriff Fred Akshar after salmonella outbreak at county jail
entry.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Broome County and Broome County Sheriff Fred Akshar after a massive Salmonella outbreak at the Broome County Jail.

entry.title: Here's the No. 1 breakfast mistake Americans make every morning, says oncologist and longevity expert
entry.summary: Ezekiel J. Emanuel has spent 35 years as an oncologist and expert on health policy. He shares his breakfast tips for better metabolic health, energy and longevity.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Surprising Anti-Aging Power Hidden in Aged Garlic
entry.summary: A compound in aged garlic extract may help reduce age-related muscle decline by activating anti-aging pathways connecting fat tissue, the brain, and muscles. Can a compound found in a common kitchen ingredient help protect muscle health as people age? New res…

entry.title: Gut Bacteria Have Been Throwing Off Calorie Counts For 130 Years: Scientists May Have A Fix
entry.summary: Gut microbes may be skewing calorie counts. Scientists built a new model that outperforms the 130-year-old Atwater formula.

entry.title: 8 High-Protein Snacks That Aren’t Cottage Cheese
entry.summary: Looking for snacks with more protein than cottage cheese? Learn 8 high-protein options, from Greek yogurt and eggs to edamame and tuna.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Blood Sugar When You Drink a Smoothie Every Morning
entry.summary: Smoothies raise blood sugar, but adding fat, protein and fiber helps prevent spikes. Dietitians share how to assemble a blood sugar-friendly smoothie.

entry.title: A silent kidney crisis is spreading far faster than experts expected
entry.summary: A sweeping global study found that chronic kidney disease now affects nearly 800 million people and has become one of the world's leading causes of death. Often silent in its early stages, the condition is also a major contributor to heart disease and ma…
